parameters.query:
UPDATE created_computers 
SET glpi_computer_id = $1,
    status = 'created',
    created_at = NOW()
WHERE id = (
  SELECT MAX(id) FROM created_computers WHERE glpi_computer_id IS NULL
);

INSERT INTO created_computers 
  (computer_name, inventory_number, serial_number, fog_host_id, glpi_data, status, prepared_at)
VALUES 
  ($1, $2, $3, $4, to_jsonb($5::json), $6, NOW())
RETURNING id, computer_name, inventory_number, serial_number, fog_host_id, glpi_data, status;

nodes.name: Prepare GLPI Request with PostgreSQL ID

parameters.query:
SELECT entity_type, glpi_id, normalized_name 
FROM glpi_cache;

SELECT 
  COALESCE(MAX(CAST(RIGHT(inventory_number, 4) AS INTEGER)), 0) as max_sequence_today
FROM created_computers 
WHERE inventory_number LIKE '6' || to_char(NOW(), 'FMMMDD') || '%';

SELECT 
  $1::integer AS glpi_computer_id,
  $2::integer AS postgres_id,
  (glpi_data::jsonb -> '_component_ids' ->> 'processor')::integer AS processor_id,
  (glpi_data::jsonb -> '_component_ids' ->> 'memory')::integer AS memory_id,
  (glpi_data::jsonb -> '_component_ids' ->> 'storage')::integer AS storage_id
FROM created_computers 
WHERE id = $2;

main.node: Prepare GLPI Request with PostgreSQL ID

json.name: <Computer #####>
json.comment:
This was formerly a scrap machine; I've combined parts and repaired the motherboard to turn it in to something we can sell.
Keyboard ordered - 21/04/26
Order number: 02-14546-07843

json.name: Computer 00004
json.comment:
Screen flickering and major display problems.
SSD changed, and now issues appear to be resolved.
